summary: Large character-select-screen decompilation slice for src/melee/mn/mncharsel. The PR replaced multiple `/// #` placeholders with C for CSS door updates, cursor think logic, random/loaded character selection, character-model motion, start/readiness checks, and nametag-list scrolling, plus header/static/type updates needed to support those functions. decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code at 61.40% (+0.07%, +2904 bytes), with new 100% matches for `fn_80262F44`, `mnCharSel_8025EE8C`, and `mnCharSel_8025D5AC`, and large partial-match gains for `mnCharSel_CursorThink`, `mnCharSel_8025DB34`, `fn_802633B0`, `fn_8025F0E0`, and `fn_80262648`. Human PR text and review comments were minimal, so intent beyond the diff and bot report is not evidenced.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work: +2821/-29 lines, added C implementations and matching tweaks for character select callbacks and helpers.
Updated prototypes from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to typed signatures for several newly-decompiled functions.
Added extern/static globals and initializer padding/fields needed by mncharsel state and matching.
Adjusted CSS struct layouts, including `CSSDoorsData` and `CSSDoorsData2` field types/bytes.

what_changed: Added required includes for trig/controller/dobj/mobj/gobjgxlink/random, `gm_1601`, and `lb_00B0`, enabling the new CSS logic to call HSD pad, animation, material, random, and game-mode helpers.

Introduced `struct CSSCursorData` and a static `struct CSSCharModel` plus pointer arrays `mnCharSel_804A0BC0[4]` and `mnCharSel_804A0BD0[4]`, replacing a small placeholder `asdf` BSS struct near the bottom of the file.

Implemented `mnCharSel_8025DB34`, which updates one CSS door: name text, duplicate costume resolution, player/CPU/none door state, handicap/CPU slider animation, player indicator, team/FFA color visuals, text hiding, game player color, and costume model animation.
Tweaked `mnCharSel_8025D5AC` with alias temporaries and an empty conditional, and wrapped `mnCharSel_8025DAA0` in `#pragma dont_inline`; the bot report says `mnCharSel_8025D5AC` became a 100% match.
Changed `mnCharSel_8025EE8C` to use a temporary `HSD_JObj* cc0` around `lb_80011E24`/`HSD_ForeachAnim`; the bot report says this function reached 100%.
Implemented `fn_8025F0E0`, a broad CSS/menu think callback that updates icon animation timers, door and slider timers, name-list/page scrolling, costume button alpha, and JObj animation.

Implemented `mnCharSel_8025FB50` for random character selection and `mnCharSel_8025FDEC` for syncing a door's selected icon from saved player `c_kind`; both update model position/linkage, icon animation timer, SFX, and duplicate costumes.
Changed `mnCharSel_CostumeChange` to use the inline `isDuplicateCostume` helper instead of calling `mnCharSel_8025DAA0` directly in the X/Y costume loops.

Implemented a large `mnCharSel_CursorThink` body handling pad input, cursor movement, cursor disconnect state, tag-list scrolling, grabbing/releasing character tokens, CPU/handicap sliders, team/mode toggles, costume changes, and start-state transitions.

Implemented `fn_80262648`, which updates the character model/token object: hide rules, port/team color frame selection, random collision avoidance between visible models, bounds clamping to icon rectangles, and smooth translation.
Implemented `fn_80262F44`, which controls ready/start prompt visibility and start-button acceptance; decomp.dev reported it as a new 100% match.

Implemented `fn_802633B0` with a source comment identifying it as a nametag-list think callback; it manages list open/close states, scroll force, visible rows, selected tag/default-name behavior, KO star reset logic, new-tag transition, and handicap slider refresh.

Updated `mncharsel.h` prototypes for `fn_8025F0E0`, `mnCharSel_8025FB50`, `mnCharSel_8025FDEC`, `fn_80262648`, `fn_80262F44`, and `fn_802633B0` to use `HSD_GObj*`, `u8`, `s32`, and concrete return types.

Added static/extern data in `mncharsel.static.h`, including `mnCharSel_804D50C8`, `mnCharSel_804D50CC`, `mnCharSel_804D50D0`, `mnCharSel_804D50D8`, `mnCharSel_804D50E0`, `mnCharSel_804D6CCC`, `mnCharSel_804D6CF3`, and `mnCharSel_804D6CF7`.
Adjusted `types.h`: `CSSDoorsData` gains `scroll_flag`, changes `xcf` and `xd3` from `int` to `float`, and `CSSDoorsData2` gains `stocks`, `xf6`, and `xf7` bytes.
smart_moves: Used rough but offset-aware local structs (`CSSCursorData`, `CSSCharModel`) to make massive `gobj->user_data` routines decompilable without pretending all fields were semantically known.

Preserved uncertain semantics by keeping many address-based or `xNN` names, while adding useful known names where the behavior was clear, such as the comment `Nametag list think callback - handles scrolling through nametag list` for `fn_802633B0`.

Separated matching and readability: `isDuplicateCostume` remains an inline helper for `mnCharSel_CostumeChange`, while `mnCharSel_8025DAA0` was protected with `#pragma dont_inline` to control codegen.

Used targeted codegen nudges instead of broad rewrites: temporary aliases (`cc0`, `new_var`, `new_var2`), empty branch padding, function-pointer temporaries, explicit casts, and `PAD_STACK` macros appear only around problem areas.
Kept header prototypes in sync with newly-decompiled bodies, reducing future `UNK_PARAMS` spread in mncharsel users.

Modeled CSS mode differences explicitly: 1P player remapping through `mnCharSel_804D6CF0`/`mnCharSel_804D6CF1`, training/two-door behavior, four-door VS loops, and team color remaps through `mnCharSel_804D50E0`/color arrays.
Let the bot report validate incremental value: exact matches were small but important, while large partial functions still captured thousands of bytes of formerly unmatched mncharsel code.
decomp_lessons: CSS/menu matching is highly sensitive to HSD animation call shape: repeated `lb_80011E24` followed by `HSD_ForeachAnim`, `HSD_JObjAnimAll`, and `HSD_AObjStopAnim` must preserve masks such as `TOBJ_MASK`, `MOBJ_MASK`, and `ALL_TYPE_MASK`.

For menu state structs, byte-vs-float layout fixes matter. The `CSSDoorsData` change from integer `xcf`/`xd3` to floats plus an inserted `scroll_flag` is a good reminder to infer type widths from arithmetic and HSD/SIS usage, not just padding.
One-player CSS code often does not index player data by door directly; future work should check the `door != 0 ? mnCharSel_804D6CF1 : mnCharSel_804D6CF0` pattern before simplifying.
Sound effects in this area are often gated by menu transition state (`mnCharSel_804D6CF6 != 3 && != 4`), so decompiling selection/random/costume logic should preserve those guards.
Large UI callbacks can be landed as valuable partial matches when exact semantics are still uncertain, as long as helper structs, prototypes, and matching artifacts are clearly localized.

Random select and loaded-character sync share downstream responsibilities: update `c_kind`, choose a valid `CSSIcon`, reposition/link the character model GObj, trigger icon animation, resolve duplicate costumes, and play character SFX.

Nametag code interacts with both UI text and game state; selecting default/no tag may reset KO stars depending on match type, so future renames should keep `xA`, `use_tag`, and `ko_star_counts` behavior connected.
naming_conventions: `mnCharSel_8025....` address-prefixed names are used for mncharsel functions whose exact semantic names are not yet assigned.
`fn_8025....` names remain for unknown callbacks even when the function body is decompiled, e.g. `fn_8025F0E0`, `fn_80262648`, `fn_80262F44`, and `fn_802633B0`.
CSS-specific type names are introduced where the subsystem is clear: `CSSCursorData`, `CSSCharModel`, `CSSDoor`, `CSSTagData`, `CSSIcon`, `CSSDoorsData`, and `CSSDoorsData2`.
Unknown struct members retain offset-style names such as `x4`, `x5`, `x8`, `xA`, `xC`, `xcf`, `xf6`, and `xf7`, often with offset comments in new structs.
Static/data globals retain doldecomp address-style symbols such as `mnCharSel_804A0BC0`, `mnCharSel_804A0BD0`, `mnCharSel_804D6CF3`, and `mnCharSel_803F0DFC`.
Typed callback prototypes use `HSD_GObj*` once known; previously unknown signatures in `mncharsel.h` were replaced with concrete return/argument types.
Existing enum-like constants such as `TRAINING_MODE` and `VS_CAMERA` are used where available, while raw constants remain where the diff did not establish names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `#pragma dont_inline on/off` wraps `mnCharSel_8025DAA0` to prevent unwanted inlining after nearby decomp work.
`#pragma auto_inline off/on` wraps `mnCharSel_8025FDEC`, indicating inline-control was part of matching that helper.
`PAD_STACK` appears in large or sensitive functions, including `mnCharSel_8025DB34`, `fn_8025F0E0`, `mnCharSel_CursorThink`, `fn_80262F44`, and `fn_802633B0`.

Alias temporaries are used to alter generated code without changing behavior: `cc0` in `mnCharSel_8025EE8C`, `new_var`/`new_var2` in `mnCharSel_8025D5AC`, and `sp5C`/`sp60` function pointers for `HSD_AObjReqAnim`/`HSD_AObjStopAnim` in `fn_8025F0E0`.
A deliberately empty condition `if (((!sp54) && (!sp54)) && (!sp54)){}` was inserted in `mnCharSel_8025D5AC`, a clear codegen-matching artifact.
Explicit casts to `u8`, `s8`, and `s32` are pervasive in door/player/mode logic and should not be casually removed; they likely affect comparisons and sign extension.
Pointer casts preserve inferred data layout, such as `icons = (CSSIcon*) (&mnCharSel_803F0A48 + 1)` and text access through `HSD_Text*`/SIS functions.
Float constants are exact and repeated (`0.0002f`, `1.25f`, `3.4f`, `-3.0f`, `31.0f`, `248.0f`, `0.5f`, `1.0f`), so future matching should preserve spelling/type where codegen depends on it.

review_feedback.feedback: Only says `Tracking PR as usual`; no detailed human rationale was provided.
Reported 3 new matches: `fn_80262F44` (+1040 bytes), `mnCharSel_8025EE8C` (+75 bytes), and `mnCharSel_8025D5AC` (+5 bytes).
Reported 10 unmatched-item improvements, notably `mnCharSel_CursorThink` to 89.29%, `mnCharSel_8025DB34` to 87.80%, `fn_802633B0` to 87.66%, `fn_8025F0E0` to 93.36%, and `fn_80262648` to 96.72%.
No human review comments or review records were present, so no reviewer naming or style guidance is evidenced.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `CSSCursorData` or `CSSCharModel` fields from `x4`/`x5`/`xC`/`x10` to semantic names.
Find later PRs that take `mnCharSel_CursorThink`, `mnCharSel_8025DB34`, `fn_802633B0`, `fn_8025F0E0`, or `fn_80262648` from partial match to 100%.
Search for later changes to `CSSDoorsData.scroll_flag`, `xcf`, and `xd3` to confirm the float/text-scroll layout.
Search for later cleanup of matching artifacts in `mnCharSel_8025D5AC`, especially `new_var`, `new_var2`, and the empty `if`.
Search for later naming or type improvements around `mnCharSel_804A0BC0`, `mnCharSel_804A0BD0`, and `mnCharSel_804D6CF7`.
Search for future implementation or cleanup around `mnCharSel_802640A0`, whose placeholder line was removed here but whose header prototype remained unknown in this slice.
